problem_statement: Vibe coding (iteratively developing software by conversing with an LLM) accumulates technical debt, security issues, and code churn because LLMs cannot reconcile accumulating human-imposed constraints over long horizons, leaving code inconsistent and buggy without effective oversight.
magnitude_evidence: a recent repository-level evaluation of 318 benchmark programs shows that the strongest model generates secure and correct code at a rate of only 28% [SecRepoBench, Dilgren et al. 2025]
insufficiency_evidence: Multi-turn coding benchmarks show that performance steeply declines compared to single-turn tasks, dominated by failures to globalize changes; long-term recall is unreliable and degrades under complexity; and 'even a high pass rate cannot guarantee the absence of important classes of bugs'
key_constructs: Type I / Type II / Type III systems
Exhaustive Switch / Discriminated Union / Union Alias / satisfies Guard
